---
title: "Assignment - Week 02"
author: "Flurin Schindele"
date: "2026-02-28"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r import-packages}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(AnnotationHub) # to fetch genomes, annotations, etc.
  library(ensembldb)     # to manipulate ensembl gene annotations
  library(GenomicRanges) # data structure for genomic ranges
  library(epiwraps)      # visualization
})
```

## Finding annotation data using `AnnotationHub`

```{r query-mouse-ensdb}
ah <- AnnotationHub()

query_results <- query(ah, c("Mus Musculus", "GRCm38", "102", "EnsDB"))
query_results

mm_ensdb <- ah[[rev(query_results$ah_id)[1]]]
mm_ensdb <- ah[["AH89211"]]
mm_ensdb
```

```{r query-mouse-genome}
query_results <- query(ah, c("Mus Musculus", "GRCm38", "dna_sm", "2bit"))
mcols(query_results)[, c("title", "genome", "rdatadateadded")]
```

This way we have 19 records and there is no clear difference other than the release date. Maybe querying for the version number (102) narrows it down.

Now we indeed have only one so we will choose this genome sequence.

```{r download-mouse-genome}
query_results <- query(ah, c("Mus Musculus", "GRCm38", "dna_sm", "2bit", "102"))
query_results

mm_genseq <- ah[[rev(query_results$ah_id)[1]]]
mm_genseq
```

When looking for the drosophila genome sequence there are again multiple entries so we will choose the most recent (using the `tail` function we specify the last entry).

```{r download-drosophila-genome}
query_results <- query(ah,
  c("drosophila melanogaster", "BDGP6", "dna_sm", "2bit")
)
mcols(query_results)[, c("title", "genome", "rdatadateadded")]

dm_genseq <- ah[[tail(rev(query_results$ah_id)[1])]]
dm_genseq
```

## Exploring mouse Ensembl data

First we want to look at protein-coding genes.

```{r filter-protein-coding-genes}
protein_genes <- genes(mm_ensdb)[
  genes(mm_ensdb)$gene_biotype == "protein_coding"
]
```

There are `r length(unique(protein_genes$gene_id))` gene ids and `r length(unique(protein_genes$symbol))` gene symbols for protein-coding genes in the database.
Some gene symbols are therefore not unique.

```{r count-gene-ids-symbols}
head(protein_genes$gene_id)
length(protein_genes$gene_id)
length(unique(protein_genes$gene_id))

head(protein_genes$symbol)
length(protein_genes$symbol)
length(unique(protein_genes$symbol))
```

We can now query for all exons that belong to protein-coding genes and group them by transcript.
By summing the width of the exons we get the length of the spliced transcripts.

```{r compute-spliced-tx-lengths}
mm_exons <- exonsBy(mm_ensdb,
  by = "tx",
  filter = ~gene_biotype == "protein_coding"
)

head(width(mm_exons))
protein_tx_spliced_len <- sum(width(mm_exons))
head(protein_tx_spliced_len)
```

```{r histogram-tx-len, out.width="75%", fig.align="center"}
hist(protein_tx_spliced_len,
  breaks = 50,
  main = "Distribution of spliced lengths of protein-coding transcripts",
  xlab = "spliced transcript length in bp)"
)
```

```{r longest-transcripts}
head(sort(protein_tx_spliced_len, decreasing = TRUE), 10)
```

Few transcripts are very long which skews the distribution and makes the plot not very informative.
The longest transcript (ENSMUST00000020488) is 123'179 bps long. Interestingly, the last exon - exon 4 - makes up most of the transcript and is 122'802 bps long ([source](https://www.ensembl.org/Mus_musculus/Transcript/Exons?db=core;g=ENSMUSG00000020255;r=10:83196085-83324369;t=ENSMUST00000020488)).
This can also be seen in the diagram below from the ensembl website. Filled boxes mark translated regions and unfilled boxes show UTRs.

<figure style="text-align: center;">
  <img src="https://static.ensembl.org/img-tmp/temporary/2026_02_28/NGRWPLWMOFTBeBaIKEAAAeLB.png" style="width: 100%;" />
  <figcaption>ENSMUST00000020488 transcript structure (source: Ensembl). Filled boxes mark translated regions, unfilled boxes show UTRs.</figcaption>
</figure>

The coding region is only 558 bps long which seems reasonable.

```{r cds-length-example}
cds <- cdsBy(mm_ensdb, by = "tx", filter = TxIdFilter("ENSMUST00000020488"))
sum(width(cds[["ENSMUST00000020488"]]))
```

In order to visualize the transcript length distribution a bit more reasonably we can perform a log-transform of the data.

```{r log-histogram-tx-len, out.width="75%", fig.align="center"}
hist(log10(protein_tx_spliced_len),
  breaks = 50,
  main = "Distribution of spliced lengths of protein-coding transcripts",
  xlab = "log10(spliced transcript length in bp)"
)
```

```{r sessionInfo}
sessionInfo()
```
